skill.name: invoice-ocr-sorter
skill.description: Automatically extract invoice data from screenshots and PDFs using OCR, then classify and export to Excel spreadsheets.
skill.summary: OCR-based invoice sorting: upload images or PDFs, get categorized data exported to Excel automatically.
skill.author: Hermes-Sedimentary

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged two HIGH patterns, both confirmed as false positives after AI evaluation. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' hit on SKILL.md:4 is a Chinese description string with no crypto code. The 'high file entropy' hit on SKILL.md:1 is expected for CJK (Chinese) character content, not obfuscation. This skill is a simple prompt template with no scripts, network calls, filesystem operations, or executable code of any kind. No prompt injection or malicious intent detected.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak cryptographic algorithm (static)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged line 4 for a weak cryptographic algorithm pattern. The line contains only a Chinese description string ('发票/账单自动整理 — 截图/PDF上传→OCR识别→分类→Excel表格') with no cryptographic code whatsoever. This is a known false positive likely caused by pattern matching on CJK character sequences that coincidentally resemble base64 or hash-like patterns.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: High file entropy (static)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged the file for high entropy (6.18 bits), suggesting possible binary or encrypted content. This is expected and normal for files containing CJK (Chinese) characters, which have higher per-character entropy than Latin/ASCII text. The file is a plain-text Markdown skill definition with no obfuscated, binary, or encrypted content.

content.user_title: Automate Invoice OCR Sorting and Excel Export
content.value_statement: Sorting invoices manually from screenshots and PDFs is slow and error-prone. This skill uses OCR to automatically extract invoice data, categorize entries, and export results as organized Excel spreadsheets.
actual_capabilities: Extract text from uploaded invoice screenshots and PDF documents using vision-based OCR
Identify key invoice fields including amount, date, vendor name, and invoice number
Categorize invoices by type such as travel, office supplies, meals, and utilities
Generate structured tabular data ready for Excel spreadsheet export
Handle both Chinese and English invoice formats
Process multiple invoices in a single batch for bulk sorting
limitations: OCR accuracy depends on image quality; blurry or low-resolution images may produce errors
Does not integrate with external accounting software or ERP systems directly
Handwritten invoices may not be recognized reliably
Output requires human review before use in official financial records

use_cases.title: Freelancer Expense Tracking
use_cases.description: A freelancer uploads monthly expense receipts as screenshots and gets a categorized Excel sheet ready for tax filing.

use_cases.title: Small Business Reimbursement Processing
use_cases.description: A small business owner collects employee reimbursement invoices throughout the month and processes them in one batch for accounting.
use_cases.target_user: Small business owners and office managers

use_cases.title: Personal Finance Organization
use_cases.description: An individual user scans shopping receipts and bills to track monthly spending by category in a single spreadsheet.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Single Invoice Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Please extract all key information from this invoice image: vendor name, date, amount, invoice number, and category. Format the result as a table row.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Invoice Sorting
prompt_templates.prompt: I am uploading multiple invoice images. For each invoice, extract the vendor, date, amount, and category. Then sort all results by date and output as a table.

prompt_templates.title: PDF Invoice Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: This PDF contains one or more invoices. Extract all invoice data including line items where available, then organize into categories and generate an Excel-ready table.
prompt_templates.scenario: Extracting data from a multi-page PDF

prompt_templates.title: Custom Category Classification
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these invoices and classify each into one of the following custom categories: office supplies, travel, meals, software subscriptions, utilities. For each invoice, provide vendor, date, amount, and your suggested category with a brief justification.

output_examples.input: User uploads a restaurant receipt screenshot from a business lunch.
User uploads three invoice screenshots from different vendors and asks for a sorted table.

best_practices: Upload clear, well-lit images with the full invoice visible for best OCR accuracy.
Review extracted amounts and dates before exporting to verify OCR correctness.
Use consistent category names across sessions to maintain clean financial records.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on OCR output as your sole financial record without manual review.
Do not upload invoices containing sensitive personal identification numbers unnecessarily.
Do not expect the skill to perform tax calculations or legal compliance checks.

faq.question: What types of invoices can this skill process?
faq.answer: It handles standard invoices, receipts, and bills in Chinese and English formats, from screenshots or PDF files.

faq.question: Can it process handwritten invoices?
faq.answer: Handwritten text recognition is limited and unreliable. Printed or digital invoices produce the best results.

faq.question: How many invoices can I process at once?
faq.answer: You can upload multiple images or a multi-page PDF in a single request. Processing time increases with the number of invoices.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to my accounting software?
faq.answer: No, this skill does not integrate with external accounting systems. It outputs data as tables that you can manually import into your preferred tool.

faq.question: Is my invoice data stored or shared?
faq.answer: Invoice images are processed within the AI conversation session. No data is stored permanently or shared with third parties by the skill itself.

faq.question: What languages does the OCR support?
faq.answer: The skill primarily supports Chinese and English text on invoices. Other languages may have reduced accuracy.
